tipRules.tip: KVD: 'Late winter/cold spring with snowmelt runoff makes water clearer than normal — great prespawn jerkbait period. Target flats near deep water access, not shallow creek backs. Focus on northerly flats that get the longest sun benefit. Dark/muck bottoms, rocky areas, dying vegetation make them warmer.'

KVD: 'In shallower prespawn areas around cover, a Rage Blade (bladed jig), swim jig, Red Eye Shad, or squarebill are my choices. These cover the shallower zone while jerkbaits handle the staging areas on the flat.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Daylight length and time of year affect spawning movements as much as anything, but conditions must be right too. Snowmelt runoff is colder AND clearer than normal spring rain — moves slower and drops water temps faster. In prespawn, target pockets and bays near creek channels or deep water access. Northerly flats with dark/muck bottoms, rock, dying vegetation are degrees warmer.'
